articles.headline: How GE Aerospace Engines Stack Up Against The Rolls-Royce Trent Series
articles.blurb: Which will come out on top?
articles.takeaway: GE and Rolls-Royce dominate wide-body engine competition, with aircraft OEM selections increasingly driven by fuel efficiency gains and supply chain resilience post-pandemic.

articles.headline: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon
articles.blurb: NASA has awarded Intuitive Machines of Houston, $180.4 million to deliver NASA-funded science and technology to the lunar surface as part of the agency’s CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) initiative and Artemis program. This lunar delivery, which includes seven payloads — five of them NASA’s...
articles.takeaway: NASA's $180.4M contract with Intuitive Machines signals accelerating commercialization of lunar logistics, reducing agency reliance on traditional aerospace contractors for Artemis infrastructure delivery.

articles.headline: 7 Reasons Why The US Air Force Still Operates The B-52
articles.blurb: From nuclear deterrence to missile truck missions, the B-52 Stratofortress remains essential to US airpower even in 2026.
articles.takeaway: The B-52's multi-mission flexibility and unmatched payload capacity make it irreplaceable despite 70+ years of service, justifying continued modernization over costly new platform development.

articles.headline: Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk
articles.blurb: Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).
articles.takeaway: Organizations must document how they selected specific controls and justified accepting remaining risk, creating an auditable decision trail for regulators under Part 5 requirements.

articles.headline: Deaf Woman Says Disability Caused Flight Removal, But Frontier Airlines Says She Had Alcohol
articles.blurb: Viral Frontier removal dispute pits disability-accommodation claims against the airline’s alcohol accusation.
articles.takeaway: Airlines face increasing litigation risk when removing passengers with disabilities, as disability claims may complicate alcohol-related enforcement and create reputational exposure.

articles.headline: The 5 Longest US Domestic Flights In 2026
articles.blurb: All five start or end in Hawaii.
articles.takeaway: Hawaii's geographic isolation makes it the only US market where ultra-long domestic routes remain economically viable despite fuel costs and crew regulations.

articles.headline: Sitting Comfortably? The Top 10 Busiest US Regional Jet Airports
articles.blurb: Chicago O'Hare reigns supreme as the busiest airport for regional flights in the US, with over 128,000 takeoffs and landings in Q2 2026.
articles.takeaway: Regional jet hubs concentrated at major metros create competitive advantages for carriers operating efficient feed networks into legacy hub structures.

articles.headline: Safety Culture in Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.
articles.takeaway: Strong SMS effectiveness depends less on compliance procedures than on psychological safety that encourages frontline employees to voluntarily report hazards without fear of punishment.

articles.headline: NASA’s Environment and Energy “Blue Marble” Awards Categories
articles.blurb: Category I: NASA Environmental Quality Award Recognizes excellence in environmental management and planning, including stewardship of natural and cultural resources. This category highlights achievements in compliance, conservation, remediation, communication, and environmental information...
articles.takeaway: NASA's environmental awards framework incentivizes aerospace facilities to integrate sustainability practices that can influence industry-wide standards for reducing aviation's ecological footprint.

articles.headline: EASA warns non-airworthy stolen engine parts could re-enter the market
articles.blurb: The European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) is warning airlines, maintenance providers and parts buyers to be on…...
articles.takeaway: Stolen engine parts from scrapped aircraft may be disguised and sold as serviceable inventory, requiring enhanced supplier verification protocols beyond standard compliance checks.

articles.headline: Inside The Airbus A350's Avionics Bay
articles.blurb: The aircraft is one of the world's most advanced machines.
articles.takeaway: The A350's distributed avionics architecture enables real-time redundancy and modular upgrades, reducing maintenance downtime while supporting next-generation autonomous flight capabilities.

articles.headline: From the Flight Deck – Frederick Municipal Airport, MD (FDK)
articles.blurb: Frederick Municipal is a small general and corporate aviation airport located just east of the city of Frederick, Maryland. The traffic mix runs the spectrum from single engine training aircraft up...
articles.takeaway: Frederick Municipal demonstrates how regional airports balance mixed traffic from flight training to corporate operations, supporting community aviation connectivity.

articles.headline: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Associate Members
articles.blurb: March 27, 2026 – Washington, D.C.—The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) is pleased to welcome Astrolab and Zeno Power, two innovative companies advancing planetary mobility and reliable energy in extreme environments. […]...
articles.takeaway: Astrolab and Zeno Power's addition signals CSF's expanding focus beyond launch services toward lunar surface operations and power systems critical for sustained deep-space missions.

articles.headline: Put Them On Blast: TMZ Asks Public For Photos Of Vacationing Lawmakers Amid TSA Crisis
articles.blurb: The shutdown, now in its 41st day, has left around 50,000 TSA officers without a paycheck.
articles.takeaway: Extended government shutdown threatens aviation security operations as unpaid TSA officers face financial hardship, potentially compromising screening consistency and airline reliability.

articles.headline: ESA to decide by June on Europe’s Gateway contributions
articles.blurb: MUNICH — For more than a year, questions have swirled about Artemis changes and the Gateway’s role in U.S. lunar ambitions. Following NASA’s March 24 decision to halt work on Gateway, the lunar-orbiting station that had been intended to support astronauts before and after lunar surface missions,...
articles.takeaway: Europe's aerospace sector faces June deadline to commit Gateway funding amid NASA's strategic pivot away from the lunar-orbiting station, creating uncertainty for ESA's Artemis participation strategy.

articles.headline: From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)
articles.blurb: Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...
articles.takeaway: Eastern Iowa Airport's mixed-use model combining flight training with commercial air carrier operations creates operational complexity that requires careful traffic management and runway coordination.

articles.headline: American Airlines Strongly Considers Bringing Back Seatback Screens, Eyes Starlink
articles.blurb: American Airlines may bring back seatback screens while exploring Starlink WiFi, signaling a shift toward enhanced inflight connectivity.
articles.takeaway: American Airlines' seatback screen reversal reflects cabin connectivity improvements making screen viability economical again, unlike previous cost-cutting rationales.

articles.headline: Gallery: Luke Days 2026
articles.blurb: A glimpse back at last weekend's air show at Luke Air Force Base in Arizona.
articles.takeaway: Luke Air Force Base air shows attract significant public engagement and demonstrate modern military aviation capabilities to civilian audiences in the Southwest region.

articles.headline: NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier
articles.blurb: Description This image captured by U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR on Nov. 10, 2025, shows Washington’s Mount Rainier. The image is cropped from a much larger swath spanning the Pacific Northwest on a cloudy day; NISAR’s L-band SAR instrument is able to peer through the clouds at the surface...
articles.takeaway: NISAR's L-band SAR capability to penetrate cloud cover provides aviation weather monitoring with unprecedented surface detail for terrain-critical operations in mountainous regions.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across all aviation system layers offers immediate environmental gains while capacity expansion technology development remains critical for handling projected traffic growth.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: NISAR Views Mount St. Helens
articles.blurb: Description This image captured by U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR on Nov. 10, 2025, shows Washington’s Mount St. Helens. The image is cropped from a much larger swath spanning the Pacific Northwest on a cloudy day; NISAR’s L-band SAR instrument is able to peer through the clouds at the surface...
articles.takeaway: NISAR's L-band SAR capability to penetrate cloud cover enables persistent volcano monitoring critical for aviation safety and volcanic ash hazard prediction.

articles.headline: NTSB Issues Preliminary Report on Hudson River Ditching
articles.blurb: Investigators say an instructional flight ended after a loss of oil pressure and total engine failure.
articles.takeaway: The ditching highlights critical gaps in engine monitoring systems, as total failure resulted from oil pressure loss that should have triggered earlier warning mechanisms.

articles.headline: How Premium Economy Has Changed Over The Last 10 Years
articles.blurb: The transformation of premium economy from a niche offering to a central pillar of airline strategy has reshaped how passengers travel.
articles.takeaway: Premium economy's evolution from niche to mainstream has become airlines' primary margin-expansion strategy, allowing them to capture price-sensitive business travelers without cannibalizing first-class revenue.

articles.headline: NASA Tech and Science Bound for Low Earth Orbit on Commercial Launch
articles.blurb: Technology and science demonstrations, supported by various NASA industry collaborations and agency developments, are set to launch to low Earth orbit aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket as part of the company’s Transporter-16 commercial rideshare mission. These demonstrations will test thermal...
articles.takeaway: NASA's reliance on commercial rideshare missions reduces standalone launch costs and accelerates technology validation cycles for aerospace development programs.

articles.headline: House Transportation Chair Sam Graves To Retire
articles.blurb: Graves helped shape FAA reauthorization, BasicMed and several other general aviation measures.
articles.takeaway: Graves' retirement removes a key advocate for general aviation regulatory flexibility, potentially shifting congressional focus on FAA modernization priorities.

articles.headline: NASA Names Scientists to Support Lunar South Pole Science
articles.blurb: NASA has selected 10 participating scientists to help shape a science plan for astronauts to complete on the lunar surface under the Artemis program – including deploying scientific instruments, making critical observations of the landing site, and collecting Moon rocks. “Congratulations to the...
articles.takeaway: NASA's selection of 10 scientists to design lunar surface operations signals that Artemis missions will prioritize rigorous scientific methodology alongside exploration, requiring aerospace contractors to develop specialized equipment and sample collection systems.

articles.headline: Three killed in Hawaii tour helicopter crash on Kauai
articles.blurb: Three people died and two others were injured after an Airborne Aviation tour helicopter crashed on the afternoon…...
articles.takeaway: Tour helicopter operations in Hawaii continue facing safety scrutiny as this latest fatal accident adds to a pattern of crashes in the commercial sightseeing sector.

articles.headline: I Am Artemis: Michael Guzman
articles.blurb: Listen to this audio excerpt from Michael Guzman, Artemis II main propulsion systems engineer: A clue to what Mike Guzman, main propulsion systems engineer at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida, loves most can be found in the signature of his work email: a complex string of equations for rocket...
articles.takeaway: NASA's Artemis propulsion engineers combine deep technical expertise with personal passion, critical for the complex system engineering required for lunar missions.

articles.headline: SES meoSphere will be core to new MEO/LEO inflight connectivity
articles.blurb: WASHINGTON D.C. — Rapidly putting the wheels in motion to launch a next-generation Medium Earth Orbit satellite network, dubbed meoSphere, SES intends to pair the new service with Low Earth Orbit satcom to power multi-orbit MEO/LEO inflight connectivity for airlines, company CEO Adel Al-Saleh...
articles.takeaway: SES's hybrid MEO/LEO meoSphere network addresses connectivity gaps by combining medium and low earth orbit satellites, enabling airlines to achieve more reliable global inflight broadband with reduced latency than single-orbit solutions.

articles.headline: This firmware update makes Avata 360 ready for real flying
articles.blurb: The new DJI Avata 360 drone is arriving with a feature-rich day-one firmware update that significantly expands what this immersive FPV drone can do right out of the box. The initial firmware rollout, headlined by v01.00.0100 for the aircraft, arrives alongside updates for the entire ecosystem,...
articles.takeaway: DJI's day-one firmware update for Avata 360 enables expanded operational capabilities immediately upon delivery, reducing post-purchase configuration time for FPV drone operators.

articles.headline: The World’s Largest Air Forces By Number Of Pilots
articles.blurb: Which air force has the deepest roster?
articles.takeaway: Pilot inventory correlates directly with operational capacity and strategic deterrence, making roster depth a critical metric for assessing national air force readiness.

articles.headline: India’s Bellatrix raises $20 million following overseas expansion drive
articles.blurb: India-based Bellatrix Aerospace announced March 27 it has raised $20 million to ramp up production of its satellite propulsion systems after securing its first large commercial customer outside the country.
articles.takeaway: Bellatrix's international customer acquisition validates Indian satellite propulsion technology commercially, signaling emerging competition in a traditionally Western-dominated aerospace supply sector.

articles.headline: NextSTEP-3 E: Network Extension for User Continuity and Sustainability (NEXUS) Ka-Band Backward-Compatible Relay Broad
articles.blurb: Synopsis | 03/23/26 https://sam.gov/workspace/contract/opp/e33cd0cc61064a6497a55fca8e9b30c6/view NASA intends to release a BAA under Next Space Technologies for Exploration Partnerships (NextSTEP-3), Appendix E, for Project NEXUS, Ka-band Backward- Compatible Relay. As the aging Tracking and Data...
articles.takeaway: NASA's NEXUS Ka-band relay will maintain backward compatibility with legacy space communications infrastructure while modernizing relay capabilities for sustained deep-space operations.

articles.headline: DJI Fly update adds support for new Avata 360 drone
articles.blurb: The DJI Fly app has officially been updated to version 1.20.0, adding support for the newly launched Avata 360 drone. The update also confirms Avata 360’s compatibility with the DJI RC 2, DJI Goggles 3, and DJI Goggles N3. more…...
articles.takeaway: DJI's ecosystem expansion through app compatibility updates demonstrates how drone manufacturers consolidate market control by requiring users to adopt standardized hardware and software platforms.

articles.headline: The $2B blind spot: Why ‘good enough’ is killing airline profitability
articles.blurb: AeroTime columnist Ann Cederhall is an instructor with IATA on Airline Distribution Strategy and with Aeroclass on Airline Retailing, Ann is a frequent speaker and…...
articles.takeaway: Airlines are leaving billions in revenue on the table by accepting suboptimal distribution and retailing strategies rather than maximizing ancillary revenue opportunities.

articles.headline: Tennessee Airport Boards Face Possible State Takeover
articles.blurb: Senate panel advances measure to replace existing airport authority boards with state appointees.
articles.takeaway: State-level governance consolidation could reshape operational autonomy and funding structures at Tennessee's regional airports, affecting local economic development priorities.
